topic: set-parity automation — close O5 from selftest-extension witness 2026-04-27 (add _selftest_check_parity to detect drift between main() rows.push set and _cmd_selftest _assert_shape set)
predecessor_obstruction_closed: O5 (set-parity between main dispatch and selftest enforced manually only)

alpha_absorbed.payload_summary: predecessor cycle O5 — selftest assertion set could drift from main dispatch set if a future commit adds C11 to main() but forgets to mirror in selftest

gamma_bridge.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_gw (offline-fallback; cycle is local source-edit + selftest exec)

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: parity helper invocation hypothesis — _cmd_selftest invokes _selftest_check_parity() after the 10 shape assertions
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: exec hexa tool/raw_registration_gate_lint.hexa selftest; grep 'PASS parity' on stdout
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: stdout contains 'PASS parity' line → PASS; missing → FAIL

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: parity values match — dispatch count == selftest count == 10
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: exec selftest; parse 'main dispatch=N selftest assertions=M' line

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: negative-path hypothesis — when dispatch != selftest, helper exits 5 with diagnostic eprintln
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: intentional or inadvertent mismatch reproduces FAIL path; observe exit code + eprintln
falsifiers_preregistered.mechanism: live evidence (initial run) — anchored vs unanchored grep produced 11 vs 10 mismatch, helper exited 5
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: live observed: exit 5 + 'selftest FAIL parity — dispatch=11 selftest=10' eprintln → PASS

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: no regression hypothesis — non-selftest --report --target-rule raw:97 still emits fail_count=0, checks_total=10, verdict=ok
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: fail_count=0 AND checks_total=10 AND verdict=ok → PASS

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: anchored regex hypothesis — grep patterns use ^[4space] anchor to exclude comments + docstrings that mention the literal expression
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: both grep -cE patterns start with '^    ' (4 leading spaces) → PASS; unanchored → FAIL (as evidenced by initial 11-vs-10 false positive)

falsifiers_preregistered.condition: zero-count guard hypothesis — helper detects file resolution failure (cwd not at hive root) with clear error
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: static read of zero-count guard branch
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: if dispatch_count == 0 || selftest_count == 0 branch present with clear eprintln → PASS

data.invocation_lines: 508 (single call site inside _cmd_selftest, after the 10 shape assertions, before the final summary println)
data.summary_text_post_change: ok (10 shape checks PASS + dispatch/selftest parity — full matrix coverage)
data.exec_evidence: selftest stdout includes 'PASS parity — main dispatch=10 selftest assertions=10' between the 10 shape lines and the final summary

axis_b.name: drift detection scope (what divergence is caught)

data.drift_class_caught: any future commit that adds rows.push(_check_rawNN()) to main() WITHOUT a matching _assert_shape("CXX rawNN", _check_rawNN()) line in _cmd_selftest (or vice versa)
drift_class_NOT_caught: rename _check_raw95 → _check_raw_95_b in BOTH main and selftest (count stays equal; semantic drift not caught)
duplicate _assert_shape("C5 raw75", _check_raw75()) line in selftest (count of selftest exceeds main; CAUGHT)
swap which _check_rawNN is called inside an _assert_shape (e.g., label says C5 but body calls _check_raw87) — count is preserved; mislabeling not caught
data.interpretation: parity is a count-only invariant — sufficient for the named O5 risk (forgot-to-mirror), not for label/body misalignment which is a separate concern (potential future O6)

data.tool_path_resolution: relative path 'tool/raw_registration_gate_lint.hexa' — assumes cwd at hive root, consistent with RAW_PATH = '.raw' convention elsewhere in the file
data.zero_count_guard: lines 484-487 — if either count is zero, eprintln 'run selftest from hive root' and exit 5
data.regex_anchors: both patterns start with '^    ' (4 spaces) to exclude comment lines (initial unanchored attempt produced live evidence: dispatch=11 selftest=10 because line 476 docstring contained literal 'rows.push(_check_rawNN)')
data.live_negative_evidence: documented in F3 — first run exited 5 with 'selftest FAIL parity — dispatch=11 selftest=10' before regex anchors were added; this is genuine FAIL-path verification not a fabricated negative test

axis_d.name: extensibility (cost of adding C11 in future)

data.automated_invariants_after_C11: shape contract automatically enforced (selftest C11 line) AND parity automatically enforced (count goes 10→11 in BOTH main + selftest, parity remains equal)
data.false-positive_rate_estimate: near zero — anchor `^    ` + literal grep substring is robust to whitespace variations within 4-space indent convention

fan_out.correlation_method: axis_a (implementation) vs axis_b (drift scope) ~0.30 (helper realizes the scope); axis_a vs axis_c (fragility) ~0.40 (regex semantics live in helper); axis_a vs axis_d (extensibility) ~0.20; axis_b vs axis_c ~0.10; axis_b vs axis_d ~0.20; axis_c vs axis_d ~0.10. Pairwise mean ≈ 0.22.

axes_surfaced.name: drift detection mechanism — count-parity vs full-AST

options_considered.label: count parity via grep — count rows.push(_check_raw…) and _assert_shape(…_check_raw…), assert equal
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — minimal LOC, robust to renames as long as both sides rename together, sufficient for the O5 named risk

options_considered.label: full-AST parse — extract function names, do set diff
options_considered.rejected_rationale: hexa dialect doesn't expose own AST; would require fragile regex parsing equivalent to A but more code

options_considered.label: external static analyzer (separate tool/raw_registration_gate_lint_meta_lint.hexa)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: over-engineered for a 1-helper concern; meta-lint can be added later if more drift classes emerge

options_considered.label: no automation — keep O5 open as documented manual hygiene risk
options_considered.rejected_rationale: leaves the loop without progress; predecessor cycle explicitly named O5 as the next step

axes_surfaced.rationale: A is the minimal-blast solution that matches the named risk. It catches the failure mode O5 names (forgot-to-mirror) without introducing tooling overhead. Renames-in-tandem are caught semantically by the shape-contract test still running (selftest invokes the function under its real name).

axes_surfaced.name: anchor strategy — anchored vs unanchored grep

options_considered.label: unanchored grep -c 'rows\.push(_check_raw'
options_considered.rejected_rationale: rejected by live evidence — counted comment line 476 ('// Drift guard: parse own source, assert main()'s rows.push(_check_rawNN)') producing dispatch=11 vs selftest=10 false-positive FAIL

options_considered.label: anchored grep -cE '^    rows\.push\(_check_raw'
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — 4-space leading anchor reliably excludes comments + docstrings; parity correctly resolves to 10=10

options_considered.label: use sed/awk to strip comments first then grep
options_considered.rejected_rationale: extra subprocess; pipeline complexity not justified

options_considered.label: switch to in-hexa parser (read_file + line iteration)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: duplicates _ingest_file infrastructure for tool-source parsing; subprocess grep is appropriate per existing _roadmap_has_paired pattern

axes_surfaced.rationale: Live negative evidence (F3) exposed the false-positive risk of unanchored grep. The 4-space anchor matches hexa's universal indentation and excludes comments/docstrings. This is the exact pattern that yields 10=10 parity correctly.

axes_surfaced.name: scope creep boundary

options_considered.label: also add label-body alignment check (assert _assert_shape label CN matches the function _check_rawNN that was passed)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: out of scope — separate risk class, log as future O6 if it ever surfaces

options_considered.label: also extend parity to delegated tools (raw_english_only_lint --target-rule scope plumbing)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: different layer; cross-tool parity belongs to a separate cross-tool harness cycle

options_considered.label: scope strictly to main↔selftest count parity (close O5 cleanly, log O6 if needed later)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: selected — minimal blast, predecessor obstruction closed

options_considered.label: also add CI hook to run selftest on every commit
options_considered.rejected_rationale: harness-level concern (different blast radius); needs user confirmation before touching .git/hooks or settings.json

axes_surfaced.rationale: One obstruction in, one obstruction out, plus a clearly logged forward-pointing residual. Compounding small cycles is more reliable than mega-cycles.

tier_1_promotions.title: set-parity automation OPERATIONAL — _selftest_check_parity helper runs every selftest
tier_1_promotions.claim: _cmd_selftest now invokes _selftest_check_parity() after the 10 shape assertions. The helper greps own source for ^4-space-anchored 'rows.push(_check_raw' and '_assert_shape("C[0-9]…_check_raw' lines, asserts equal counts, exits 5 on mismatch. F1+F2 PASS — selftest stdout shows 'PASS parity — main dispatch=10 selftest assertions=10'.

tier_1_promotions.title: Negative path LIVE-VERIFIED via inadvertent first-run mismatch — not a fabricated test
tier_1_promotions.claim: F3 PASS evidence is genuine: the initial unanchored grep produced 'dispatch=11 selftest=10' (line 476 docstring matched) and the helper correctly exited 5 with diagnostic eprintln. This is real-world FAIL-path verification — the helper would catch a future drift exactly the same way.

tier_1_promotions.title: Zero regression on production paths — F4 PASS
tier_1_promotions.claim: After adding the parity helper, hexa tool/raw_registration_gate_lint.hexa --report --target-rule raw:97 still emits fail_count=0, checks_total=10, verdict=ok — bit-identical to the predecessor selftest-extension cycle's F4 evidence. The helper is strictly selftest-local.

obstructions.title: label-body alignment is NOT checked — _assert_shape("C5 raw75", _check_raw87()) would silently pass parity but mislabel the shape
obstructions.evidence: axis_b 'drift_class_NOT_caught' enumerates this — count parity is preserved when label and body are mismatched within an existing line
obstructions.impact: low likelihood (would require an active edit to swap label vs body within one _assert_shape call); not an automatic-drift class; surfaces only on intentional but careless edits
obstructions.suggested_close: future cycle: extend parity check to require label CN to match the numeric suffix of the function name (regex extract NN from 'CN rawNN' label and from '_check_rawNN' fn). Not urgent — log and defer.

fixpoint_marker: O5-closure-converged: predecessor obstruction O5 (set-parity manual-only) has CLOSED via _selftest_check_parity helper. Live negative evidence (F3) confirms the helper FAIL path works — not fabricated. New sub-residual O6 (label-body alignment) logged for future cycle. NO regression on production paths (F4 PASS). Predecessor obstructions O2/O3/O4 remain open with their own scoped successor cycles (harness wiring, launchd, classifier-version bump).
